---
title: "Results"
page-layout: full
date: "April 11, 2026"
date-modified: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Project Resources

[View GitHub Repository](https://github.com/cakemerc01/IS428_G2_VA-Project){.btn .btn-primary target="_blank"}
<!-- [Tableau Dashboard: Task 1](https://public.tableau.com/your-link-1){.btn .btn-secondary target="_blank"}
[Tableau Dashboard: Task 2](https://public.tableau.com/your-link-2){.btn .btn-secondary target="_blank"}
[Tableau Dashboard: Task 3](https://public.tableau.com/your-link-3){.btn .btn-secondary target="_blank"} -->
:::

---

# Task 1: Understanding Sailor Shift's Career Profile

### Who has she been most influenced by over time?

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](Images/performer_sankey.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"}
![](Images/lyricist_sankey.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](Images/producer_sankey.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"}
![](Images/composer_sankey.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"}
:::
:::

To map Sailor Shift’s professional network, we started with her early work in the band Ivy Echoes. From there, we identified key collaborators such as Maya Jenson, Sophie Ramirez, Jade Thompson, and Lila “Lilly” Hartman. We then expanded the analysis to include the composers and lyricists she has worked with across her career.

By constructing an ego network with Sailor at the center, an interesting pattern emerged. While most artists in the network are referenced through specific songs or albums, Sailor Shift is referenced as an individual. This suggests that her influence goes beyond her body of work. She functions as a creative archetype. In other words, her personal style and identity serve as a blueprint that other artists and groups draw from.


### Who has she collaborated with and directly or indirectly influenced?

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](Images/Band Members.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"}
![](Images/Collaborators.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](Images/Ego Network.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"}
:::
:::

To map Sailor Shift’s professional network, we started with her early work in the band Ivy Echoes. From there, we identified key collaborators such as Maya Jenson, Sophie Ramirez, Jade Thompson, and Lila “Lilly” Hartman. We then expanded the analysis to include the composers and lyricists she has worked with across her career.

By constructing an ego network with Sailor at the center, an interesting pattern emerged. While most artists in the network are referenced through specific songs or albums, Sailor Shift is referenced as an individual. This suggests that her influence goes beyond her body of work. She functions as a creative archetype. In other words, her personal style and identity serve as a blueprint that other artists and groups draw from.


### How has she influenced collaborators of the broader Oceanus Folk community?

![](Images/Collaborators1c.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](Images/Daniel.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](Images/Beatrice.png){fig-align="center" width="100%"}
:::
:::

Sailor Shift’s impact on the Oceanus Folk community can be seen both in her direct collaborations and in wider industry trends. By analyzing the release timelines of her collaborators, we found that her debut acted as a turning point. It brought veteran artists out of long hiatuses and encouraged newer musicians, such as Beatrice Albright and Daniel O’Connell, to focus their work on the Oceanus Folk style.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](Images/2020.png){fig-align="center" width="90%" fig-cap="2020}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](Images/2030.png){fig-align="center" width="90%" fig-cap="2030}
:::
:::

This individual influence aligns with a larger shift in the music industry. While Dreampop was dominant during Sailor’s rise in the late 2020s, the 2030s saw Oceanus Folk surge to become the leading genre. The strong correlation between Sailor’s career trajectory and this genre’s growth suggests that she played a key role in shaping Oceanus Folk’s rise.

---

# Task 2

---
